notes: SHIP v1.14.246 — HONEST 'UNKNOWN' TYPE: the app stops calling real hardware a blanking panel. John's call, taken INSTEAD of the handoff's Ship 2 (site profiles), on the systemic finding surfaced by the .245 BLANK-token audit. THE LIE: `_TMAP.other = 'blank'` mapped master_hostType's fallthrough ('I could not classify this device') onto the flat type 'blank' — whose colour is slate #22303e and whose label is literally 'BLANK'. So EVERY unclassified Master device rendered as a blanking panel: the app drew 'nothing is installed here' over real, racked hardware. That is 459 hosts on SPARKS (11.1%) and 1 on DFW02. Same honesty class as the 1U-height guess that .238 exists to kill, but strictly WORSE: a wrong height still SHOWS a device; BLANK ERASES it. A gloved tech reading the aisle would walk past 448 InfiniBand switches that the screen says are empty U. FIVE EDITS, ALL INSIDE forge3d_render, ALL DISPLAY-ONLY. (1) TYPE_COLOR — added `unknown: '#ffcb45'` (gold: the app's existing 'we don't know something' language, same token MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN already uses). 'blank' KEEPS slate #22303e. The two claims must never share a colour. (2) TLABEL — added `unknown: 'UNKNOWN'`. (3) _TMAP — `other: 'blank'` -> `other: 'unknown'`. (4) `_TMAP[s.type] || 'blank'` (:17857) -> `|| 'unknown'` — an UNMAPPED key is by definition unknown, never empty. This is belt-and-braces on the exact trap that made the .245 spec inert: that `|| 'blank'` is what swallowed the 'server' fix and rendered a UFM as a blanking panel. (5) flagsOf — new case: type 'unknown' -> 'DEVICE TYPE UNKNOWN · <model> — REAL GEAR, NOT A BLANKING PANEL'. Gold WARN family (row-warn + ⚠ + .detail-flag), NOT the red conflict family — an unclassified device is a warning, a U-collision is danger. ZERO CSS (row-warn/.detail-flag already exist). THE DESIGN LINE — WHY 'unknown' IS DRAWN TO SCALE AND NOT HATCHED. drawGuts reserves the hazard hatch + hard border + ⚠ for `bad` (conflict/overflow) and `unk` (hgtUnknown). Those all mean ONE thing: DO NOT TRUST THIS GEOMETRY. Type-unknown is a DIFFERENT claim — the U-span IS true (we read it straight from the Master; for net-6x100g-02 the height is even vendor-seeded at 1U per .242), we simply cannot name the device. So it renders SOLID, AT CORRECT SCALE, with a gold type-spine and a detail flag — and NO hatch, because hatching it would falsely assert the geometry is untrustworthy. Both flags can co-exist and often do (q3400-ra is BOTH type- and height-unknown -> gold spine AND hatch). Getting this line wrong in either direction would be a new lie, which is the whole point of the ship. NOT TOUCHED (strict scope): `deploy_classifyDevice`'s 'blank' fallthrough (the EDP/CSV path). Unlike _TMAP it is PERSISTED (deployment.edpParsed.racks[].slots.type), so flipping it changes seeded deployment records and needs its own ship + its own verify. _TMAP is DISPLAY-ONLY — verified: mscope_buildRacksFromSnapshot persists master_hostType's RAW output ('other'), and _TMAP exists only inside forge3d_render (2 references, both display). Nothing persisted changes this ship. Counts are also untouched: refreshCounts uses slots.length and statusOf is orthogonal to type, and `blank` was never special-cased anywhere in the Forge module (no skip in drawGuts, no exclusion from any denominator) — grep-confirmed before designing. So the RACKED/PENDING burn-down is bit-identical. PRE-SHIP GUARD (both real masters replayed through the SHIPPED maps, extracted verbatim from the edited file, on the app's own vendored SheetJS): DFW02 — 2347 placed hosts, flat-type census {switch:905, gpu:1441, unknown:1}, moved vs .245 = 1 (blank->unknown), STILL RENDERING BLANK = 0. SPARKS — 4143 placed hosts, census {gpu:1681, pdu:1296, switch:693, unknown:459, patch:12, server:2}, moved vs .245 = 459 (blank->unknown), STILL RENDERING BLANK = 0. NOT ONE host carrying a real type moved. Invariants asserted green: unknown colour != blank colour (#ffcb45 vs #22303e); every _TMAP value resolves to a defined TLABEL *and* TYPE_COLOR (0 undefined-label rows — the failure mode that would print the literal string 'undefined' on a tray). NET EFFECT: zero devices sourced from a Master are described as blanking panels any more. 'blank' remains a legitimate, reachable type — but only from the EDP path, where it means a device the data POSITIVELY identifies as a blanking panel. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS brace balance 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS this ship), CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0), three-stamp lockstep .245 -> .246. ?legacy=1: unaffected — every edit is inside forge3d_render, and #forge3d-sheet is CSS-gated to body.rd ('body.rd #forge3d-sheet.open{display:flex}'), so legacy cannot render the Forge scene at all. BATCH = 2 (.245 .246), cap 6. ⚠️ .245 is still UNVERIFIED — John directed this ship ahead of its device pass; flagged per CALL 0. DEVICE-VERIFY (John), one pass covers both: [.246] SPARKS s3:176 -> U31 metal-jump01 / U29 pkey02 / U28 pkey04 now read UNKNOWN in gold, NOT BLANK · tap each -> gold flag line 'DEVICE TYPE UNKNOWN · net-6x100g-02 — REAL GEAR, NOT A BLANKING PANEL' · they are drawn SOLID and to scale (no hatch) · s1:001 -> the 448 q3400-ra InfiniBand switches read UNKNOWN + gold, and (being height-unknown too) still carry the hatch + 'MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN' · DFW02 c1:002 unchanged (its 2 known collisions still red) · no tray anywhere prints the literal word 'undefined' · RACKED/PENDING counts unchanged from .245. [.245] s3:176 U27 UFM still reads SERVER. [both] ?legacy=1 curl-diff. STILL OWED, JOHN'S RULING (unchanged by this ship — it makes them HONEST, it does not classify them): q3400-ra x448 (every one named s*-ib-ruNN-*, i.e. the master itself calls them InfiniBand — strong, but a NAMING INFERENCE, so NOT seeded) · net-6x100g-02 x6 · fs-media-converter-chassis x5 · DFW02's one empty-model row @c1:001:38.
